ayahs.textArabic: حمٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hameem
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Ha, Meem.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ḥâ-Mĩm.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلۡكِتَٰبِ ٱلۡمُبِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalkitabi almubeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By the clear Book,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By the clear Book!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّآ أَنزَلۡنَٰهُ فِي لَيۡلَةٖ مُّبَٰرَكَةٍۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا مُنذِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna anzalnahu fee laylatin mubarakatin inna kunna munthireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We sent it down during a blessed night. Indeed, We were to warn [mankind].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We sent it down on a blessed night, for We always warn ˹against evil˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فِيهَا يُفۡرَقُ كُلُّ أَمۡرٍ حَكِيمٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Feeha yufraqu kullu amrin hakeemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On that night is made distinct every precise matter -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that night every matter of wisdom is ordained

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡرٗا مِّنۡ عِندِنَآۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا مُرۡسِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Amran min AAindina inna kunna mursileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Every] matter [proceeding] from Us. Indeed, We were to send [a messenger]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: by a command from Us, for We have always sent ˹messengers˺

ayahs.textArabic: رَحۡمَةٗ مِّن رَّبِّكَۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلۡعَلِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rahmatan min rabbika innahu huwa alssameeAAu alAAaleemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As mercy from your Lord. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as a mercy from your Lord. He ˹alone˺ is truly the All-Hearing, All-Knowing—

ayahs.textArabic: رَبِّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَآۖ إِن كُنتُم مُّوقِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rabbi alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma in kuntum mooqineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Lord of the heavens and the earth and that between them, if you would be certain.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, if only you had sure faith.

ayahs.textArabic: لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ يُحۡيِۦ وَيُمِيتُۖ رَبُّكُمۡ وَرَبُّ ءَابَآئِكُمُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La ilaha illa huwa yuhyee wayumeetu rabbukum warabbu abaikumu alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: There is no deity except Him; He gives life and causes death. [He is] your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Him. He ˹alone˺ gives life and causes death. ˹He is˺ your Lord, and the Lord of your forefathers.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ هُمۡ فِي شَكّٖ يَلۡعَبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal hum fee shakkin yalAAaboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they are in doubt, amusing themselves.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, they are in doubt, amusing themselves.

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱرۡتَقِبۡ يَوۡمَ تَأۡتِي ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِدُخَانٖ مُّبِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fairtaqib yawma tatee alssamao bidukhanin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then watch for the Day when the sky will bring a visible smoke.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Wait then ˹O Prophet˺ for the day ˹when˺ the sky will be veiled in haze, clearly visible,

ayahs.textArabic: يَغۡشَى ٱلنَّاسَۖ هَٰذَا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yaghsha alnnasa hatha AAathabun aleemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Covering the people; this is a painful torment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: overwhelming the people. ˹They will cry,˺ “This is a painful torment.

ayahs.textArabic: رَّبَّنَا ٱكۡشِفۡ عَنَّا ٱلۡعَذَابَ إِنَّا مُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rabbana ikshif AAanna alAAathaba inna muminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They will say], "Our Lord, remove from us the torment; indeed, we are believers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Our Lord! Remove ˹this˺ torment from us, ˹and˺ we will certainly believe.”

ayahs.textArabic: أَنَّىٰ لَهُمُ ٱلذِّكۡرَىٰ وَقَدۡ جَآءَهُمۡ رَسُولٞ مُّبِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Anna lahumu alththikra waqad jaahum rasoolun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: How will there be for them a reminder [at that time]? And there had come to them a clear Messenger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: How can they be reminded when a messenger has already come to them, making things clear,

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ تَوَلَّوۡاْ عَنۡهُ وَقَالُواْ مُعَلَّمٞ مَّجۡنُونٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma tawallaw AAanhu waqaloo muAAallamun majnoonun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then they turned away from him and said, "[He was] taught [and is] a madman."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then they turned away from him, saying, “A madman, taught by others!”?

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا كَاشِفُواْ ٱلۡعَذَابِ قَلِيلًاۚ إِنَّكُمۡ عَآئِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna kashifoo alAAathabi qaleelan innakum AAaidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We will remove the torment for a little. Indeed, you [disbelievers] will return [to disbelief].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We will remove ˹that˺ torment for a while, and you ˹Meccans˺ will return ˹to disbelief˺.

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ نَبۡطِشُ ٱلۡبَطۡشَةَ ٱلۡكُبۡرَىٰٓ إِنَّا مُنتَقِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma nabtishu albatshata alkubra inna muntaqimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day We will strike with the greatest assault, indeed, We will take retribution.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Then˺ on the Day We will deal ˹you˺ the fiercest blow, We will surely inflict punishment.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَلَقَدۡ فَتَنَّا قَبۡلَهُمۡ قَوۡمَ فِرۡعَوۡنَ وَجَآءَهُمۡ رَسُولٞ كَرِيمٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad fatanna qablahum qawma firAAawna wajaahum rasoolun kareemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had already tried before them the people of Pharaoh, and there came to them a noble messenger,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, before them We tested Pharaoh’s people: a noble messenger came to them,

ayahs.textArabic: أَنۡ أَدُّوٓاْ إِلَيَّ عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِۖ إِنِّي لَكُمۡ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: An addoo ilayya AAibada Allahi innee lakum rasoolun ameenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Saying], "Render to me the servants of Allah. Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger,"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹proclaiming,˺ “Hand over the servants of Allah to me. I am truly a trustworthy messenger to you.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَن لَّا تَعۡلُواْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِۖ إِنِّيٓ ءَاتِيكُم بِسُلۡطَٰنٖ مُّبِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waan la taAAloo AAala Allahi innee ateekum bisultanin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [saying], "Be not haughty with Allah. Indeed, I have come to you with clear authority.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And do not be arrogant with Allah. I have certainly come to you with a compelling proof.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنِّي عُذۡتُ بِرَبِّي وَرَبِّكُمۡ أَن تَرۡجُمُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnee AAuthtu birabbee warabbikum an tarjumooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, I have sought refuge in my Lord and your Lord, lest you stone me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord so you do not stone me ˹to death˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِن لَّمۡ تُؤۡمِنُواْ لِي فَٱعۡتَزِلُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wain lam tuminoo lee faiAAtazilooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But if you do not believe me, then leave me alone."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹But˺ if you do not believe me, then let me be.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَدَعَا رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنَّ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ قَوۡمٞ مُّجۡرِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FadaAAa rabbahu anna haolai qawmun mujrimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [finally] he called to his Lord that these were a criminal people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ultimately, he cried out to his Lord, “These are a wicked people!”

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَسۡرِ بِعِبَادِي لَيۡلًا إِنَّكُم مُّتَّبَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faasri biAAibadee laylan innakum muttabaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Allah said], "Then set out with My servants by night. Indeed, you are to be pursued.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Allah responded,˺ “Leave with My servants at night, for you will surely be pursued.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱتۡرُكِ ٱلۡبَحۡرَ رَهۡوًاۖ إِنَّهُمۡ جُندٞ مُّغۡرَقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waotruki albahra rahwan innahum jundun mughraqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And leave the sea in stillness. Indeed, they are an army to be drowned."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And leave the sea parted, for they are certainly an army bound to drown.”

ayahs.textArabic: كَمۡ تَرَكُواْ مِن جَنَّٰتٖ وَعُيُونٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kam tarakoo min jannatin waAAuyoonin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: How much they left behind of gardens and springs
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Imagine˺ how many gardens and springs the tyrants left behind,

ayahs.textArabic: وَزُرُوعٖ وَمَقَامٖ كَرِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WazurooAAin wamaqamin kareemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And crops and noble sites
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as well as ˹various˺ crops and splendid residences,

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَعۡمَةٖ كَانُواْ فِيهَا فَٰكِهِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WanaAAmatin kanoo feeha fakiheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And comfort wherein they were amused.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and luxuries which they fully enjoyed.

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَٰلِكَۖ وَأَوۡرَثۡنَٰهَا قَوۡمًا ءَاخَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kathalika waawrathnaha qawman akhareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Thus. And We caused to inherit it another people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So it was. And We awarded it ˹all˺ to another people.

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَا بَكَتۡ عَلَيۡهِمُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ وَٱلۡأَرۡضُ وَمَا كَانُواْ مُنظَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fama bakat AAalayhimu alssamao waalardu wama kanoo munthareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the heaven and earth wept not for them, nor were they reprieved.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Neither heaven nor earth wept over them, nor was their fate delayed.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ نَجَّيۡنَا بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ مِنَ ٱلۡعَذَابِ ٱلۡمُهِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad najjayna banee israeela mina alAAathabi almuheeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We certainly saved the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We certainly delivered the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment

ayahs.textArabic: مِن فِرۡعَوۡنَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَالِيٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡمُسۡرِفِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Min firAAawna innahu kana AAaliyan mina almusrifeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From Pharaoh. Indeed, he was a haughty one among the transgressors.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: of Pharaoh. He was truly a tyrant, a transgressor.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدِ ٱخۡتَرۡنَٰهُمۡ عَلَىٰ عِلۡمٍ عَلَى ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqadi ikhtarnahum AAala AAilmin AAala alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We certainly chose them by knowledge over [all] the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, We chose the Israelites knowingly above the others.

ayahs.textArabic: وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡأٓيَٰتِ مَا فِيهِ بَلَٰٓؤٞاْ مُّبِينٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waataynahum mina alayati ma feehi balaon mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We gave them of signs that in which there was a clear trial.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We showed them signs in which there was a clear test.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَيَقُولُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna haolai layaqooloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, these [disbelievers] are saying,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, these ˹Meccans˺ say,

ayahs.textArabic: إِنۡ هِيَ إِلَّا مَوۡتَتُنَا ٱلۡأُولَىٰ وَمَا نَحۡنُ بِمُنشَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In hiya illa mawtatuna aloola wama nahnu bimunshareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "There is not but our first death, and we will not be resurrected.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “There is nothing beyond our first death, and we will never be resurrected.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأۡتُواْ بِـَٔابَآئِنَآ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatoo biabaina in kuntum sadiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then bring [back] our forefathers, if you should be truthful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Bring ˹back˺ our forefathers, if what you say is true.”

ayahs.textArabic: أَهُمۡ خَيۡرٌ أَمۡ قَوۡمُ تُبَّعٖ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبۡلِهِمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَٰهُمۡۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ مُجۡرِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ahum khayrun am qawmu tubbaAAin waallatheena min qablihim ahlaknahum innahum kanoo mujrimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Are they better or the people of Tubba' and those before them? We destroyed them, [for] indeed, they were criminals.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Are they superior to the people of Tubba’ and those before them? We destroyed them ˹all˺, ˹for˺ they were truly wicked.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا لَٰعِبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama khalaqna alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma laAAibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did not create the heavens and earth and that between them in play.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything in between for sport.

ayahs.textArabic: مَا خَلَقۡنَٰهُمَآ إِلَّا بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكۡثَرَهُمۡ لَا يَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma khalaqnahuma illa bialhaqqi walakinna aktharahum la yaAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We did not create them except in truth, but most of them do not know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We only created them for a purpose, but most of these ˹pagans˺ do not know.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡفَصۡلِ مِيقَٰتُهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna yawma alfasli meeqatuhum ajmaAAeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the Day of Judgement is the appointed time for them all -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely the Day of ˹Final˺ Decision is the time appointed for all—

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ لَا يُغۡنِي مَوۡلًى عَن مَّوۡلٗى شَيۡـٔٗا وَلَا هُمۡ يُنصَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma la yughnee mawlan AAan mawlan shayan wala hum yunsaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day when no relation will avail a relation at all, nor will they be helped -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: the Day no kith or kin will be of benefit to another whatsoever, nor will they be helped,

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا مَن رَّحِمَ ٱللَّهُۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلۡعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa man rahima Allahu innahu huwa alAAazeezu alrraheemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except those [believers] on whom Allah has mercy. Indeed, He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except those shown mercy by Allah. He is truly the Almighty, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ شَجَرَتَ ٱلزَّقُّومِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna shajarata alzzaqqoomi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the tree of zaqqum
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely ˹the fruit of˺ the tree of Zaqqûm

ayahs.textArabic: طَعَامُ ٱلۡأَثِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: TaAAamu alatheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is food for the sinful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: will be the food of the evildoer.

ayahs.textArabic: كَٱلۡمُهۡلِ يَغۡلِي فِي ٱلۡبُطُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaalmuhli yaghlee fee albutooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Like murky oil, it boils within bellies
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Like molten metal, it will boil in the bellies

ayahs.textArabic: كَغَلۡيِ ٱلۡحَمِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kaghalyi alhameemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Like the boiling of scalding water.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: like the boiling of hot water.

ayahs.textArabic: خُذُوهُ فَٱعۡتِلُوهُ إِلَىٰ سَوَآءِ ٱلۡجَحِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Khuthoohu faiAAtiloohu ila sawai aljaheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It will be commanded], "Seize him and drag him into the midst of the Hellfire,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It will be said,˺ “Seize them and drag them into the depths of the Hellfire.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ صُبُّواْ فَوۡقَ رَأۡسِهِۦ مِنۡ عَذَابِ ٱلۡحَمِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma subboo fawqa rasihi min AAathabi alhameemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then pour over his head from the torment of scalding water."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then pour over their heads the torment of boiling water.”

ayahs.textArabic: ذُقۡ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلۡعَزِيزُ ٱلۡكَرِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thuq innaka anta alAAazeezu alkareemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [It will be said], "Taste! Indeed, you are the honored, the noble!
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The wicked will be told,˺ “Taste this. You mighty, noble one!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ هَٰذَا مَا كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَمۡتَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna hatha ma kuntum bihi tamtaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, this is what you used to dispute."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is truly what you ˹all˺ used to doubt.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ فِي مَقَامٍ أَمِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna almuttaqeena fee maqamin ameenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the righteous will be in a secure place;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the righteous will be in a secure place,

ayahs.textArabic: فِي جَنَّٰتٖ وَعُيُونٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fee jannatin waAAuyoonin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Within gardens and springs,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: amid Gardens and springs,

ayahs.textArabic: يَلۡبَسُونَ مِن سُندُسٖ وَإِسۡتَبۡرَقٖ مُّتَقَٰبِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yalbasoona min sundusin waistabraqin mutaqabileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Wearing [garments of] fine silk and brocade, facing each other.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: dressed in fine silk and rich brocade, facing one another.

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَٰلِكَ وَزَوَّجۡنَٰهُم بِحُورٍ عِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kathalika wazawwajnahum bihoorin AAeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Thus. And We will marry them to fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So it will be. And We will pair them to maidens with gorgeous eyes.

ayahs.textArabic: يَدۡعُونَ فِيهَا بِكُلِّ فَٰكِهَةٍ ءَامِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: YadAAoona feeha bikulli fakihatin amineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will call therein for every [kind of] fruit - safe and secure.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There they will call for every fruit in serenity.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا ٱلۡمَوۡتَ إِلَّا ٱلۡمَوۡتَةَ ٱلۡأُولَىٰۖ وَوَقَىٰهُمۡ عَذَابَ ٱلۡجَحِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yathooqoona feeha almawta illa almawtata aloola wawaqahum AAathaba aljaheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will not taste death therein except the first death, and He will have protected them from the punishment of Hellfire
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There they will never taste death, beyond the first death. And He will protect them from the punishment of the Hellfire—

ayahs.textArabic: فَضۡلٗا مِّن رَّبِّكَۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلۡفَوۡزُ ٱلۡعَظِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fadlan min rabbika thalika huwa alfawzu alAAatheemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As bounty from your Lord. That is what is the great attainment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as ˹an act of˺ grace from your Lord. That is ˹truly˺ the ultimate triumph.

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِنَّمَا يَسَّرۡنَٰهُ بِلِسَانِكَ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fainnama yassarnahu bilisanika laAAallahum yatathakkaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, We have eased the Qur'an in your tongue that they might be reminded.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We have made this ˹Quran˺ easy in your own language ˹O Prophet˺ so perhaps they will be mindful.

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱرۡتَقِبۡ إِنَّهُم مُّرۡتَقِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fairtaqib innahum murtaqiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So watch, [O Muhammad]; indeed, they are watching [for your end].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Wait then! They too are certainly waiting.
